title: #people-ops: 2890012345
preview:
Priya Natarajan: FYI team 2b — Ravi Patel will be taking a planned sabbatical starting 2026-04-15 (approx 12 weeks). To reduce single-owner risk were splitting Head of Runtime responsibilities into two interim roles: Zoe Kim as Interim Runtime Infrastructure Lead and Marcus Lee as Interim Runtime Delivery Lead. This is a planned, temporary change; Ravi will support handoffs during the next two weeks.

Ravi Patel: Thanks everyone. I'll upload handoff docs and stay available for async Qs. Appreciate Zoe & Marcus stepping in.

Zoe Kim: I'll take infra ops ownership. Priorities: Dedicated capacity planning, kernel rollout schedule, infra on-call transfer. I'll post a 7-day handoff checklist to Confluence later today.

Marcus Lee: I'll own routing, rollout cadence, and delivery ops. Planning a cross-functional sync Wed 9am PT to surface any blockers.

Patrick (Recruiting): Noted. This creates short-term hiring actions: (1) Contract Eng Lead (infra) for ~12w, (2) Senior Runtime PM (6+ month contract), and (3) coverage ICs for on-call shifts. Target: post Eng Lead by EOW, PM next week. I'll draft reqs and proposed panels.

Sam (Runtime IC): Recommend 48h paired pager overlap with current on-call and a 2-day shadow for infra contractors. Also suggest we freeze noncritical rollout windows during first 2 weeks of handoff.

Peter (Finance): Contractor budget approved up to 12w at current rates for Eng Lead. Senior PM requires director-level headcount sign-off. Please loop me before offers.

Jules (Legal): Reminder — contractors needing access to customer data or private repos require NDA + legal notice 48h prior. Coordinate with People Ops.

Priya Natarajan: Onboarding tasks for interim leads: handoff doc, pager overlaps, access pre-approvals. Kayla/People Ops will kick off access requests. Well also schedule weekly 30/60 check-ins with Ravi's skip-level sponsor.

Priya Natarajan: Recruiting Bot posted provisional reqs and links: req-9876 (Contract Eng Lead), req-9877 (Sr Runtime PM). Patrick will share interview panels and rubrics tomorrow.

Zoe Kim: I'll draft a short take-home infra exercise (20-40m) focused on capacity tradeoffs.

Marcus Lee: PM exercise will be a scenario: degraded region + model fallback, 45m.

Priya Natarajan: We'll cross-post this update to #eng-runtime, #eng-sre, and #product. If you can help with panels or handoffs, pls ping Patrick or me. This is a planned sabbatical; Ravi returns mid-July.

Ravi Patel: One ask — flag any feature flags or rollout windows that need extension during my absence in the tracking doc: https://redwoodintra/confluence/runtime-sabbatical-handoff

Patrick (Recruiting): I'll DM volunteers and send calendar invites. ETA for first contractor start ~2026-04-20.

Priya Natarajan: Thanks all for moving quickly and helping keep runtime stability high while Ravi is out :tada:

preview:
Kayla (People Ops): Quick heads-up — Maya Lin will be stepping in as Interim Head of Platform Partnerships effective Mon 4/4. We're splitting Platform Partnerships out of Growth Partnerships for clearer ownership. This creates two backfill reqs (Sr Partnerships PM + Partnerships Ops lead). Tracking doc: https://redwoodintra/confluence/platform-partnerships-reorg :tada:

Maya Lin: really excited to jump in — thanks everyone. I'll run weekly 1:1s with the existing partners team to stabilize handoffs. happy to meet folks asynchronously this week.

Priya Natarajan: congrats Maya! who will you report to during the interim? will this affect the runtime/product reporting?

Kayla (People Ops): Maya will report to Diego (Head of Platform) for the interim. Product/reporting lines remain the same for now; Diego and Maya will surface any overlaps for follow-up.

Diego Morales: adding that we'll do a 30/60/90 plan in the shared doc. no org-wide product changes today.

Allie (Hiring Manager): +1 to shadow rota. i'll volunteer Sam and Noor for initial overlap. also can we add a PM-specific take-home exercise (30-60m)?

- 30/60/90 template assigned
```

title: #people-ops: 2823456789
preview:
Maya Lopez: Quick update — Leadership approved a small 'skill mobility' program and a focused hiring wave to support Dedicated and Optimize teams. 8 reqs approved + 4 guaranteed backfills; pilot rotation pool of 5 engineers for 6-week exchanges.

Maya Lopez: Posting cadence: well publish 2-3 reqs/day starting Monday. Internal candidates get a 9-business-day priority window.

Rory Chen: Thanks Maya. Can managers nominate people for the rotation or is it opt-in only?

Maya Lopez: Both — managers can nominate and individuals can opt-in. Manager consent required for transfers. Were encouraging nominations for cross-skilling.

Samir Patel: Recruiters will put up REQs starting with REQ-6010. Internal priority first; please flag names on the hire sheet so we can coordinate offer timelines.

Ana Martinez: Any special onboarding for rotated folks? worried about getting them productive quickly.

Priya Natarajan: Receiving teams will run a 2-week immersion + 1 mentor. We'll supply a one-page handover template. Also rotated folks get a 1-week on-call deferment.

Claire Wu: Compensation note: no blanket band changes. If a role needs a market-adjusted offer, submit a comp-exception packet with data; I'll review within 72hrs.

Maya Lopez: Action items: hiring managers — update headcount & rotation-eligible column in the central sheet (https://r.infra/hire-ops) by Thu EOD. Also tag any customer-impacting tickets so we can plan coverage.

Samir Patel: Recruiter Bot: Job post created REQ-6010 (Dedicated Capacity Engineer) by Samir Patel — https://recruiter.rw/REQ-6010

Ana Martinez: Interview clustering request please — block two 90-min blocks per week to reduce callbacks.

Samir Patel: Will do. Also running an interview calibration session Wed 3pm for panels.

Maya Lopez: We'll publish an FAQ + rotation playbook and pin it in #people-ops tomorrow. Thanks everyone — this is targeted and timeboxed; reach out if you have edge cases. :sparkles:

title: #people-ops: 2765432100
preview:
People Ops: Heads up — we're making a small reorg in Platform Delivery to better align serving/runtime, release ops, and feature delivery.

People Ops: Effective Monday, Camille Ortega will step in as Interim Head of Platform Delivery for ~8 weeks while we run backfills for two senior IC roles. Camille will focus on cross-team delivery, triage cadence, and the on-call handoff.

Camille Ortega: Thanks everyone. Quick priorities: 1) stabilize on-call rotation (Tony + Meera to shadow), 2) finalize the delivery charter for the Serving ControlPlane split, 3) run a 1:1 kickoff with each tech lead this week.

Aisha Patel: Congrats Camille! How should teams log questions/risks? Slack channel or a single doc?

Camille Ortega: Please post visible items in #eng-runtime and add concise tickets to the "platform-delivery/triage" board. I'll keep a rolling weekly update in the wiki page: <https://wiki.redwood.com/platform-delivery/temporary-reorg>

Jordan Kim: Any changes to interview loops / hiring owners for the backfills?

People Ops: Hiring owners remain the same (Jordan + Priya). We've opened two reqs: Senior Backend Eng (serving) and Senior Reliability Eng. Interview panel draft shared in the hiring doc. Expect initial screens to start ~2 weeks.

Priya Natarajan: We'll prioritize internal candidates for the reliability slot; please flag recommendations to me directly.

Meera Singh: On-call shadow plan: Tony and I can cover week 1 shadow, then Camille will rotate into lead escalation. I'll drop a schedule in #eng-oncall.

Tony Li: FYI — I validated capacity for a temporary extra pager for Camille. No infra changes needed.

Camille Ortega: Thanks all — quick ask: leads, please schedule 20-min syncs before Friday so I can get context. Also ping People Ops after you do your 1:1s.

People Ops: I'll run a short intake to capture role expectations and ramp timelines. Camille, HR Bot confirmed provisioning for your accounts; you should have SSO access by EOD.

HR Bot: Camille Ortega provisioning completed: SSO, admin access to delivery board, calendar groups.

Aisha Patel: Appreciate the clarity. Will share a short risks note for my squad.

Jordan Kim: One more: is there a plan for notifying customers for any potential SLAs during the transition?

Camille Ortega: Good call — no customer-facing changes expected. If anything looks like it might hit latency/throughput SLOs we'll escalate and draft a comms plan with Customer Success.

People Ops: Thanks everyone. I'll post a summary and the hiring links after this thread. :tada:

title: #people-ops: 2765409999
preview:
Nina Alvarez: Heads-up — Executive update this morning: we're lifting the temporary hiring freeze for a targeted set of roles focused on Platform reliability and Runtime optimizations. This is a small, strategic burst not a full reopen.

Nina Alvarez: Whats changing: 9 reqs approved (5 senior ICs, 2 ICs, 2 backfills). Well also create a small satellite hiring pool for high-latency pockets (contract + FTE mix). Posting starts Tue. Internal candidates get a 7-business-day priority window.

Jordan Kim: Clarifying Qs: are these hires global or US-first? and will we allow remote-first for senior ICs?

Nina Alvarez: Global postings where possible; US-first for two roles that must be in specific regions for latency reasons. Senior ICs — remote-first is allowed pending manager approval and timezone compatibility.

Samir Patel: Recruiters will publish REQ numbers tomorrow morning (starting REQ-5566). If you have internal candidates please flag them on the HR sheet so we can fast-track screens.

Priya Natarajan: For rotations/backfills: are we doing any cross-team rotations as part of this lift?

Nina Alvarez: Yes — 3 short rotations (4-8 weeks) to move expertise into tight spots. Rotations opt-in, with manager sign-off and optional conversion at end.

Claire Wu: Compensation note: no across-the-board band changes. For market-competitive offers, submit comp exception with documented comps and a product impact justification; Ill aim to respond within 48 hours.

Recruiter Bot: New posting created: REQ-5566 (Runtime Performance Engineer) by Samir Patel  https://recruiter.rw/REQ-5566

Samir Patel: Interview cadence suggestion: cluster interviews into 3 blocks to reduce context switching. Also will run a calibration session Wed 9am for panelists.

HR Ops: Action items: hiring managers update the headcount sheet (https://r.infra/hire-sheet) and mark any "remote-eligible" flags by EOD Mon. Internal priority window is 7 business days from posting.

Jordan Kim: Any changes to on-call expectations for rotated folks? need to coordinate with SRE.

Nina Alvarez: Rotated folks may be exempt from on-call for the first week of rotation; after that, SRE and receiving manager will jointly determine coverage. Please coordinate with Priya for scheduling.

Priya Natarajan: Got it. Ill circulate a simple on-call checklist and a short handover template.

Claire Wu: Recording for audit: compile final move lists and any comp exceptions by Sep 30 so we can close approvals.

Samir Patel: Quick note on referrals: priority referral bonus applies if the hire converts within 90 days. DM me for questions.

Nina Alvarez: Thanks team. This is a deliberate, focused effort to get capacity into the areas that unblock product launches. Ping me or Jordan for edge cases. :rocket:

title: #people-ops: 2491203456
preview:
Aisha Patel: Quick announce — we’re running a 12-week Platform Rotation Fellow pilot to accelerate cross-team leadership development and cover an immediate gap in Dedicated Services. The goal: one senior engineer or manager rotates into the Senior Manager, Dedicated Services role for 12 weeks while Recruiting runs a full backfill hire. Details below.

Aisha Patel: Why — we need (1) uninterrupted ownership for the dedicated product workstream, (2) a development pathway for senior ICs to get manager experience, and (3) time to run a deliberate external search.

Aisha Patel: Timeline — rotation starts May 2, onboarding week Apr 29–May 3. Internal EOIs due Apr 12, hiring req live Apr 5, final candidate selected by Apr 19.

Aisha Patel: Scope — the fellow will inherit the Dedicated roadmap squad, be the escalation for latency/cost incidents, own sprint planning and cross-team coordination, and lead the canary rollouts for the May capacity change. This is a temporary manager seat (12 weeks) — comp band and headcount do not change.

Recruiting: We’ll post the backfill req in Greenhouse by Apr 5. We have two internal potentials in the funnel already and will supplement with targeted reach-outs to senior candidates who’ve worked in infra+serving.

Tomás Rivera: Love this program. I’ll push it to eng-leads and flag folks who’ve expressed interest in management. :+1:

Sofia Ramirez: Quick Q — is the fellow required to be in SF/NY, or is this open to remote/hybrid folks?

Aisha Patel: Remote ok — we’ll support hybrid across NA/EU. There will be a few fixed meeting times (weekly leadership sync, on-call handoff) that the fellow must attend live.

Priya Natarajan: Hiring ops note — People Ops will run a short calibration panel for internal candidates to keep this fair. We’ll also confirm comp neutrality so there’s no salary reset.

Sofia Ramirez: I’m interested — DMing you now, Aisha. Curious about the expected time commitment outside of normal sprint cadence.

Aisha Patel: Thanks Sofia — roughly +1.5–2 hrs/day the first two weeks for handoffs, then ~1 hr/day ongoing for org syncs, plus normal sprint duties. We’ll work with your manager on capacity.

Recruiting: Heads up to hiring managers — please update interview rubric to reflect manager responsibilities (1:1 coaching, stakeholder mgmt, incident leadership). We’ll share a short candidate brief template.

Tomás Rivera: I'll cover the canary rollout owner during the overlap period if needed. Happy to pair with the fellow for the first release window. :rocket:

People Ops Bot: Reminder — create rotation approval doc in /company/rotations and file the temporary role in Greenhouse by Apr 5.

Aisha Patel: Action items: (1) People Ops to post internal EOI form (by EOD), (2) Recruiting to create req (by Apr 5), (3) Managers to flag potential candidates, (4) Program leads to add a 30/60/90 for the fellow so objectives are clear.

Tomás Rivera: ✅ will add the 30/60/90 template to the program doc.

Sofia Ramirez: Thanks — excited about this pilot. :party:

title: #people-ops: 2143654789
preview:
People Ops: Heads up — quick policy update that affects international travel and PTO sync. Starting June 1, anyone traveling internationally for >5 consecutive days needs to submit the Travel Declaration form at least 7 days before departure. This is to help with emergency outreach and insurance coverage.

Asha Patel: link to form + checklist: https://intranet.redwood/travel-declare (please fill in emergency contact, itinerary, and whether you'll be using company-funded travel allowance).

Jordan Lee: Quick Q — does this apply to contractors and short-term consultants?

Asha Patel: Good question. Contractors on >90 day engagements and contractors using company travel funds are included. Short-term consultants traveling on personal funds are exempt but we strongly encourage them to share a local contact.

Ben Carter: Security note — please don't post full itineraries in public channels. If you need help sharing sensitive details, DM TravelBot or security@redwood. TravelBot will redact what needs to be redacted when routing. :lock:

Maya Gomez: PTO sync detail — if your trip overlaps with scheduled PTO, please update your PTO request to mark it as "out-of-country" so People Ops can apply the correct leave rules and benefits top-ups. We'll auto-approve same-day windows for <=3 days; longer trips need manual review.

People Ops: Also doing a short Code of Conduct refresh for travel: be mindful of local laws, respect host orgs, and follow our harassment and alcohol policies while representing Redwood. We'll run a 10-min refresher microtraining next Wednesday 09:00 PT — mandatory for anyone on international travel in the next 90 days.

Jordan Lee: Thanks — will the microtraining be recorded?

People Ops: Yes, recording + FAQ will be posted to the People Ops wiki. If you need an accommodation for timing, DM me (Asha) and we'll arrange an alternate slot.

TravelBot: Reminder scheduled: Travel Declaration form autopings assignees 7 days before departure when the form is incomplete. Use `/travel help` for commands.

Ben Carter: If you have high-risk itineraries (country list in the wiki), loop security for trip-specific briefings. We're happy to help with safe-comms kits and emergency response contacts.

Maya Gomez: TL;DR: submit form 7 days prior, mark PTO as out-of-country, attend 10-min travel CoC refresher if traveling internationally. Ping people-ops with questions. :airplane:

title: #people-ops: 2081112223
preview:
Ava Chen: hey team — quick Q: I just moved across the county line (still same state). Does this count as a QLE for dependent benefits? wondering about daycare FSA and whether payroll tax withholdings for dependents change. :house_with_garden:
People Ops: @Ava Chen welcome! moving can be a QLE only if it affects coverage or eligibility (or causes loss/gain of dependent eligibility). can you say which dependents and what changed? county -> county, same employer?
Ava Chen: same employer, dependent is my 3yo in daycare; daycare center is now in new county and the state childcare subsidy program for his daycamp is different. also wondering if I need to update W-4 or local tax forms.
Benefits Bot: Auto-reply: for midyear changes, we need to confirm (1) whether the move causes a change in eligibility for a benefit (yes/no), and (2) supporting docs within 30 days of the change. See: https://redwood.example.com/people-ops/qle-policy (internal).
Samir Patel: county-level programs can affect dependent assistance eligibility but not the company medical eligibility. FSA elections: you can change FSA only with a QLE that affects coverage or dependent status. if the subsidy change affects your out-of-pocket daycare cost, that's not alone a QLE. have you lost access to the provider network or enrollment due to geography?
Ava Chen: no network change for medical. daycare subsidy is different but daycare is still available. so sounds like maybe not a QLE?
Jorge Rivera: quick payroll note — local tax withholding can change when your address crosses municipal/county boundaries. update address in Workday and payroll will recalc withholding. W-4 state/local forms may be required depending on county.
Maya (Payroll): if you do need new local forms, attach them to a case in HR portal or email payroll@redwood.com. timeline: submit within 30 days if the change impacts tax status; FSA changes require an approved QLE (submit proof). Checklist below:
```
- Change of address (utility bill or lease, DMV)
- Proof of QLE if applicable (loss/gain of benefit due to move)
- Completed payroll/local tax form (if new municipality requires)
- Submit to hr-portal or email payroll@redwood.com within 30 days
```
People Ops: to summarize: moving counties alone usually isn't a QLE for benefits elections unless it directly changes a dependent's eligibility or coverage. you should update address + check local tax forms. if daycare subsidy loss/gain caused by county rules causes a financial change and you think it impacts coverage, open a case with docs and we'll review.
Ava Chen: got it — thanks! I'll update Workday, collect lease + subsidy letter and open an HR case if needed. :thumbsup:
Samir Patel: ping me if you want me to review the docs before you submit.
People Ops: thanks! leaving this thread open for any follow-ups.

preview:
Aisha Patel: Quick company update — we’re consolidating Infra SRE into Platform & creating two new IC manager roles to cover infra reliability and platform integrations. This is primarily to reduce duplication and speed up cross-team rollout for Dedicated and Private. Hiring plan attached: short-term backfills + two new hires. Please read and ask Qs here. link: https://redwood-internal/sr/infra-platform-plan

Priya Natarajan: Thanks Aisha. Can you confirm org reporting? will those two managers report to Platform Eng (me) or Runtime?

Aisha Patel: They’ll report to Platform Eng (Priya) but runtime dotted-cots for latency/serving expertise. We want clear ownership for infra reliability while keeping runtime alignment for kernel/latency work.

Diego Morales: Congrats folks. Any change to headcount approvals/process? need to know before we open reqs.

Aisha Patel: Good Q — headcount is pre-approved for the two manager slots and 3 immediate backfills (SRE ICs). PM/TPR approvals are done, budget scoped for Q2 hires. Recruiting will open reqs tomorrow.

HR Bot: Hiring checklist posted: /doc/hiring/infra-platform-checklist.md — includes interview loop, scorecard, and relocation guidance. Reminder: use the new inclusive interview rubric v2.

Tomas Rivera: How does this affect oncall rotation? are we folding Infra SRE oncall into Platform oncall or keeping separate for the transition window?

Priya Natarajan: Short term (30d) we’ll keep existing oncall but redistribute pages to Platform SRE shadowers. After 30d we’ll merge to single Platform oncall with two-week overlap. We’ll post exact schedule by EOD Monday.

Evan Carter: Are we reusing any open candidates from the Runtime pipeline? I have a few mid-senior folks that might fit the infra reliability role.

Aisha Patel: Yes — recruiting will keep existing pipelines open and re-tag candidates where appropriate. Evan — can you drop your shortlist in the hiring tracker? we’ll prioritize strong infra/RPC/latency backgrounds.

Diego Morales: For interviewing loops, can we add a kernel/latency tech screen? runtime folks should own that. Template below: ```Screen 1: Recruiter pre-screen
Screen 2: Kernel/Latency (runtime eng)
Screen 3: System design (infra)
Screen 4: Behavioral + leadership
```

Priya Natarajan: That looks good. Recruiting — please include a runtime engineer as interviewer for the kernel screen.

Nora Elbaz: Welcome note for new hires? Do we have a playbook for onboarding across Private/Dedicated infra?

Aisha Patel: We’re extending the Platform onboarding playbook with infra-specific modules (KV cache, batching, deployment policies). Recruiting/DevEx will coordinate to ensure new hires have early access to Dedicated labs.

HR Bot: FYI — onboarding checklist for Platform+Infra will be published to /playbooks/onboarding/platform-infra-v1 by tomorrow.

Diego Morales: One quick ask — can we prioritize candidates in EU timezone for the Dedicated oncall overlap? reduces paging latency.

Priya Natarajan: Yes, include timezone preference in req. Recruiting, add location tag EU & PST flexibility.

Aisha Patel: Thanks all. I’ll post the final hiring doc and the req templates here after recruiting opens. This thread is the canonical place for Qs; please @mention me or Priya for anything urgent. :thumbsup:

Evan Carter: Ack — adding shortlist now.

Tomas Rivera: Will follow up on the oncall schedule wording once you post.

Nora Elbaz: Thanks Aisha+Priya — excited about the consolidation.

Aisha Patel: Cheers — more updates soon.

title: #people-ops: 1999012345
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick heads-up — we finalized a small change to the parental leave benefits notice period: effective May 1, managers now need 30 days' notice for planned leaves (up from 14). This is to help staffing and Dedicated capacity planning. Please read the short FAQ I attached in the doc: https://redwood.example.com/policies/benefits-notice-period (TL;DR: emergency/exceptions still allowed).

Marcus Li: Thanks Ava — does this apply retroactively to requests already submitted but not approved?

Ava Chen: Good q — no, it won't apply retroactively. Any requests submitted before May 1 keep the prior policy. We'll add a clear note in the policy doc.

Nina (Manager): Is there a manager signoff workflow change? I want to ensure I don't miss approvals during busy sprints.

Ava Chen: Yes — small workflow tweak in HRIS: approvals now show a highlight if notice <30 days. We also added an automated reminder to the manager 2 weeks before the proposed start date.

Gus (IT): FYI — to help managers, we pushed a tiny update to the internal 'Time Off' Slack bot. Example command to see flagged requests:
```
/toff flagged --team @your-team --window 60d
```
It lists requests with notice <30 days.

Marcus Li: Nice. For the training piece you mentioned earlier — what's required?

Ava Chen: We're rolling a 15-minute mandatory microtraining on data handling & protected info for any manager who approves leaves (covers handling sensitive docs in the private deployment + redaction checklist). It's brief and interactive. Enrollment is by RSVP; deadlines staggered by team size. Link + calendar RSVP: https://lms.redwood.example.com/courses/data-micro-managers

Riley (Legal): Quick legal note: the microtraining must be completed within 21 days of the first approval action after May 1. If someone willfully bypasses it, we escalate to HR review. This is policy-adjacent, not a disciplinary trap — it's mainly to reduce accidental PII exposure.

Nina (Manager): Can you add me to the manager exception review group? I sometimes approve urgent leaves for infra oncall.

Ava Chen: Done — added you to the exception reviewers doc. Also, if you need blanket exceptions for a role/team, DM me with the justification and we'll route to Legal & Benefits.

Marcus Li: Who gets the reminder emails when someone doesn't finish the microtraining? Manager only, or manager + people-ops?

PeopleOps Bot: Reminder routing: primary reminder -> manager; escalations at 10 days post-approval -> people-ops@redwood.com and your HRBP. Calendar invites include 'complete course' link and an org-wide iCal for each cohort.

Gus (IT): Enrollment tip — if you want to bulk-enroll a team: run the script in infra-tools repo: `scripts/enroll_bulk.sh teams.csv` (requires infra-tools access). I can run for folks who can't access it.

Marcus Li: Thanks. One last: will payroll/benefits be updated to reflect the notice change in the benefits dashboard?

Ava Chen: Yes — payroll & benefits dashboard reflects the policy change starting May 1. We'll sync with Finance next week and post a short changelog entry.

Riley (Legal): Approved language for the policy doc is in the doc's bottom section. Please don't edit that portion without Legal review.

Ava Chen: Action items: 1) Managers — review the policy doc + RSVP for microtraining by April 25; 2) PeopleOps/IT — verify bot highlight + bulk-enroll flows by April 28; 3) Legal/Finance — confirm changelog entry by Apr 30. I'll post a follow-up thread for the RSVP counts tomorrow. :raised_hands:

Nina (Manager): Acknowledged. I'll RSVP and ask my leads to do the same.

Marcus Li: Acknowledged.

PeopleOps Bot: Scheduled reminder will post to this channel on 2026-04-20 09:00 UTC.

title: #people-ops: 1988888888
preview:
Aisha (People Ops): Heads up 1: people ops + eng leadership are rolling out a short hiring/ops change to speed critical backfills and standardize interview slates. Effective immediately: 1) Centralized interview rubric for IC+manager roles. 2) Two-week fast-track for priority backfills (platform/security/customer-facing). 3) Dedicated sourcer for each priority req.

Diego Morales: Can you list which roles are priority?

Aisha (People Ops): Priority list for this sprint:
- Staff SRE (backfill)
- Senior Backend Engineer, Inference Routing (backfill)
- Director, Customer Solutions (new req)
- Security Engineer, Infra (backfill)

Priya Natarajan: For the inference role, will we require kernel-level experience or is production scale LLM infra enough?

Jordan Kim: Production LLM infra + distributed systems experience is the main bar. Kernel-level is nice-to-have but not mandatory. I'll add the spec to the tracker.

Sofia Lopez: Quick ask  can we ensure at least one URM candidate per onsite? People Ops, please prioritize diverse slates.

Aisha (People Ops): Absolutely. Fast-track includes mandatory diverse slates and a two-stage fairness review before offers.

Hiring Bot: New quick-postings created: /jobs/SRE-522, /jobs/BE-INF-334, /jobs/CUSTDIR-019, /jobs/SEC-410. Tracker: https://notion.company/recruiting/fast-track-sprint

Diego Morales: How are we handling interview ownership while hiring? We don't want teams to be overloaded.

Aisha (People Ops): Two steps: 1) Short interview panels (3 people max) using the centralized rubric. 2) Interview shifts - each team provides a rota so interviews are spread. Lizzy will coordinate the rota.

Lizzy Mendoza: I'll post the rota template and take the first two weeks of interviews for platform roles. Also prepping an FAQ for managers about relocation and offer bands.

Priya Natarajan: SLA for filling backfills?

Aisha (People Ops): Target: initial screens within 7 days, onsite within 21 days, offers for top-priority roles in 5-7 weeks. We'll report weekly to execs.

Jordan Kim: I'll open the reqs and share JD drafts in the recruiting channel. Also, I'll loop in hiring managers for the panel list.

Sofia Lopez: Noted. Will coordinate candidate source partners for URM pipelines and pass along curated lists to Jordan.

Aisha (People Ops): Quick JD snippet for Staff SRE (short):
```
Staff Site Reliability Engineer
- 7+ yrs operating distributed production systems
- Experience with GPU infra, autoscaling, and incident management
- Lead cross-team reliability improvements and SLO ownership
```

Diego Morales: Thanks. One more ask  please add a short comms blurb for any teams impacted by re-routed hiring duties (so customer teams aren't surprised).

Aisha (People Ops): Will do. Comms blurb + FAQ out by EOD. If anyone sees blockers (panels, travel, compensation) flag them here or DM me. :wave:

title: #people-ops: 1967890123
preview:
Lina Gomez: manager asked what counts as promo evidence for an SDE2 -> SDE3 (EMEA), and whether retro is automatic for timezone hires. need a short checklist i can paste.
Ethan Park: short checklist below. evidence should map to rubric axes (ownership, impact, mentorship). prefer a 1-page bulleted summary + 2 manager statements with metrics or links. for EMEA comp, submit in local currency (EUR) and include USD equivalent. retro: processed if ticket approved before payroll cutoff; otherwise next cycle.
Olivia Chen: can we include a sample metric? manager wants examples to show the IC.
Ethan Park: sample metrics: "reduced p99 latency 18% on payments service (measured by traces); led cross-team rollout of caching, saved $45k/mo infra" or "owned onboarding project that cut time-to-first-query from 6d -> 2d, increased activation by 12%". tie to dates and dashboards.
payroll-bot: Payroll note: cutoff is last business day of the month for EMEA payroll; FX rate applied on effective date. Include base, bonus target, and equity details in ticket.
Lina Gomez: what's our limit for retro lookback? manager is worried a delayed packet might require big backpay.
comp-ops: policy: standard lookback 120 days; exceptions up to 1 year need director+comp signoff. retro > $12k requires finance approval. include estimate in the ticket to speed processing.
Ethan Park: small checklist to paste in Workday:
```
- Attach Promo Evidence (1-page bullets + 2 manager statements)
- Add before/after role bullets + org chart snippet
- Submit comp-change in EUR and list USD equivalent
- Add estimated retro (USD) and reason for delay
- Confirm employee uploaded local tax docs
- Tag @payroll-bot and @comp-ops
```
Lina Gomez: perfect thanks. i'll send to the manager and ask for the 1-pager today.
Olivia Chen: if they need the template i can drop a Google doc with the metric examples in #people-ops resources.

title: #people-ops: 1935123456
preview:
Ava Chen: Hi all, quick question on open enrollment — I missed the live session and just realized I need to add my spouse retroactive to Feb 1. Is that possible? Who do I kick off with and what docs do you need? :grimacing:

Marta (People Ops): hey Ava :wave: you can still add dependents mid-year for qualifying events, and some teams allow backdating for special cases. Can you confirm: was this a qualifying event (marriage, loss of other coverage, etc.)?

Ava Chen: Yes — we got married on Jan 28 but I thought the deadline to submit was later. Marriage certificate is available. Do you need a PDF of it uploaded somewhere? Also worried about premiums for Feb.

Marta (People Ops): Congrats! :tada: If marriage is the qualifying event we can process a retro addition. Please upload the marriage certificate and a completed Dependent Enrollment Form to the benefits portal within 30 days of the event. For backdating to Feb 1 we typically require the documents + a short note.

Ava Chen: Where's the benefits portal link? I couldn't find Dependent Enrollment Form.

Marta (People Ops): Portal link: https://benefits.redwood.com/login (use SSO). Form is under Benefits > Forms > "Dependent Enrollment PDF". If you prefer, you can also email people-ops@redwood.com with attachments and we'll upload on your behalf.

Jordan - Payroll: FYI on premium/proration: if we can backdate enrollment to Feb 1, premiums for Feb will be charged retroactively on the next payroll cycle as an adjustment. If you opt to add now without backdating, premiums start the following month.

Ava Chen: Good to know — thanks Jordan. If payroll posts an adjustment, will it show as "Benefits Adjustment" on my paycheck?

Jordan - Payroll: Yes, it'll appear as "Benefits Adjustment - Medical" (or dependent plan name). Payment can be deducted across 1-2 cycles depending on amount, we can set it to spread if you prefer. Ping me if you want a specific cadence.

Marta (People Ops): To speed things up, drop the PDF here or DM people-ops and ask for "retro add". We'll confirm once it's processed. Also attach spouse's insurance card or proof of loss of other coverage if relevant.

Ava Chen: Uploading now. One more: do I need to enroll in dental separately? I only saw medical options.

Sam L. (People Ops): dental is separate yes — there's a checkbox on the Dependent Enrollment Form for dental/vision. If you want spouse on dental, check that box and attach the same docs.

Benefits Bot: Reminder: Dependent Enrollment Form template v3.1.0. Required fields: dependent name, DOB, relationship, supporting doc. Upload path: /benefits/uploads/employee-id/ (SSO required).

Ava Chen: Uploaded marriage_certificate_ava_jordan.pdf and filled DependentEnrollment_ava.pdf to the portal. Let me know if anything missing.

Marta (People Ops): Got them — thanks. I'll start the retro add request. Expect a confirmation DM from me within 2 business days. Jordan, can you flag payroll for potential Feb retro premium? :eyes:

Jordan - Payroll: Yep noted, I'll watch for the ticket and queue the adjustment. Will update once I schedule it.

Ava Chen: Amazing, thank you both! Also, any note on whether this affects my HSA contributions?

Jordan - Payroll: HSA pre-tax elections may need recalculation for the year if plan tier changed. I'll include HSA adjustment in the note and provide a breakdown when we finalize the premium.

Marta (People Ops): If anything else pops up, DM people-ops. Welcome again and congrats :heart:

Ava Chen: Thanks!! :smile:

title: #people-ops: 1934556677
preview:
Priya Natarajan: Hi all, quick onboarding/security question — my apartment uses an enterprise WPA2-Enterprise cert profile (workplace Wi-Fi). Do I need to add anything special for Redwood VPN/Okta, or is the usual Okta SSO + Redwood VPN client fine? Also, when does the phishing simulation run and how often should I expect those emails? :thinking:

Ethan Brooks: Welcome Priya! Okta SSO + the Redwood VPN client is sufficient for remote access, you don't need to re-register your home AP. The only thing we require is that your company laptop is enrolled in MDM and FileVault (or BitLocker) is enabled. Phish sims typically run quarterly for most teams; completion of the related micro-training is required if you click a simulated link.

Marta Lopez: Adding on: if you plan to use a personal device for any work, you must request a personal-device exception and enroll it in MDM first. We strongly encourage using the company-issued laptop for dev work. If you want the loaner, reply here and I can kick off provisioning.

onboarding-bot: Reminder: "Security Basics" and "PhishSim Awareness" assigned to @Priya Natarajan. Security Basics due in 14 days. PhishSim micro-course auto-assigned if you interact with the simulation. Learn: https://learn.redwood/sec-basics

Priya Natarajan: Thanks! How do I verify FileVault is on? And for Windows users, BitLocker? I want to confirm before I accept the loaner.

IT Support: Commands to check: macOS:
```
sudo fdesetup status
```
Windows (PowerShell as admin):
```
Get-BitLockerVolume | ft MountPoint, ProtectionStatus
```
If the loaner needs setup, we push the MDM profile and the certs. Once enrolled, you should see the "Redwood MDM" profile in System Preferences / Security & Privacy.

Ethan Brooks: Also a heads-up: phishing sims are low-noise but realistic. If you fail one, the remediation is a short 5-10 minute interactive module. No punitive action, just required completion. If you have concerns about work email forwarding or personal mailing lists, ask People Ops for exemptions.

Priya Natarajan: Good to know. I'll take the company laptop — thanks Marta. Can someone add me to #sec-oncall and #new-joiners? My manager said they'd handle it but want to double-check.

Marta Lopez: Done — added you to both channels and submitted the loaner request (ticket #IT-7421). ETA 48 hours for pickup or courier. Scheduling note: we run a 10-minute security sync for new hires on Tuesdays and Thursdays at 9:30 AM PT. I'll put you on tomorrow's slot.

Priya Natarajan: Perfect, appreciate the quick turnaround :smile:
